title: Windows 11 KB5079391 Update Rolled Out with New Features, Offline Installer Download Links Inside
body:
A Significant Update for Windows 11 Users: KB5079391 Brings Enhanced Features and Performance

Windows 11 users have reason to rejoice as Microsoft has released the latest optional update, KB5079391, which brings several notable improvements to the operating system. This update is a significant step forward in enhancing the overall user experience, particularly for those with high-end hardware configurations. At the forefront of these enhancements is support for 1000 Hz refresh rate monitors, a feature that will undoubtedly appeal to gamers and content creators who demand the highest level of visual fidelity.

The update also introduces a key change that makes it easier for users to manage Smart App Control, a feature designed to protect users from malicious software. With KB5079391, users can now turn on or off Smart App Control without having to reinstall the operating system. This is a significant convenience, as users will no longer have to go through the hassle of reinstalling Windows 11 every time they want to toggle this feature. Furthermore, the update includes several other improvements, such as enhanced security features, performance enhancements, and bug fixes.

The inclusion of 1000 Hz refresh rate support is a notable improvement, particularly for enthusiasts who have invested in high-end gaming monitors. These monitors offer a level of visual detail and smoothness that is unmatched by lower refresh rate displays, making them ideal for gaming, video editing, and other graphics-intensive activities. By supporting these high-end monitors, Windows 11 is able to provide users with an unparalleled level of performance and visual fidelity.

The KB5079391 update is also a testament to Microsoft's ongoing commitment to improving Windows 11. The company has shown a keen interest in listening to user feedback and addressing the concerns of its user base. By releasing this update, Microsoft is demonstrating its willingness to evolve the operating system and make it more user-friendly. This update is particularly significant for users who have been waiting for improvements to Smart App Control and other features.

One area where this update falls short is its optional status. While this allows users to choose when to install the update, it also means that some users may not be aware of the benefits it offers. Microsoft has historically been criticized for its somewhat opaque update process, and this update is no exception. However, for those who do choose to install KB5079391, the benefits are clear.

As Windows 11 continues to evolve, it's likely that we'll see more updates like KB5079391 in the future. Microsoft has a clear vision for its operating system, and its commitment to improving it is evident in updates like this. With KB5079391, users can look forward to an improved user experience, enhanced performance, and a host of new features that will make Windows 11 even more appealing to enthusiasts and casual users alike. As the operating system continues to mature, we can expect to see even more significant updates that will further solidify its position as a leading platform for PC users.
